content.claim: The European Court of Human Rights issued a judgment against Hungary in March 2023 for racial segregation of Romani children in education, finding violation of right to education and non-discrimination; Hungary was ordered to desegregate and pay damages. As of 2024, the Committee of Ministers monitoring implementation reports the case remains open due to insufficient Hungarian government compliance.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Political feasibility lens
limitations: The decision remains contested in Hungarian domestic political discourse, with government officials characterizing it as violating public sentiment
Implementation monitoring relies on committee reports; specific remedial steps taken or refused are not individually itemized in these notes
The relationship between SEN identification rates and segregated placement outcomes is documented in litigation but not quantified in the research notes
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
